guardrails: The cohort has 20 direct, 20 inverse, and 20 neutral laws.
The 60 laws span mechanics, fracture, fatigue, transport, thermal, electrical, magnetic, optical, interfacial, microstructural, corrosion, fluid, and kinetic domains.
Only calibration-neutral laws define center and scale.
Direct, inverse, and validation-neutral labels never alter the normalization.
Every law crosses two equation surfaces, two cases, two numerical directions, and two answer orders.
The layer and hidden direction remain frozen from old laws.
All outputs, including failures, are retained.
matched_contrast: Within each law/surface/case/answer-order cell, frozen physical score for numerical-up minus score for numerical-down.

neutral_normalization.center: median law contrast over the 10 calibration-neutral laws
neutral_normalization.reason: defines an empirical physical null instead of assuming raw zero
neutral_normalization.scale: 1.4826 times MAD over the 10 calibration-neutral laws
neutral_normalization.validation_neutrals: the other 10 neutral laws are excluded from normalization

primary_endpoints.calibrated_sign_accuracy: direct above and inverse below calibration-neutral median
primary_endpoints.ordinal_spearman: law sign -1/0/+1 versus neutral-normalized contrast, validation neutral only
